title: Worker with conditional reschedule based on result
prompt: Write `MyApp.Workers.DataFetchWorker` (queue `:ingest`, max_attempts 5). perform/1 takes `%{"source_id" => id, "cursor" => cursor}`. It calls `MyApp.Ingest.fetch_page(id, cursor)` which returns either `{:ok, :done}` (all pages fetched), `{:ok, {:more, next_cursor}}` (another page available), or `{:error, reason}`. Logic: (1) on `{:ok, :done}` return `:ok` (no reschedule), (2) on `{:ok, {:more, next_cursor}}` enqueue a new job with `schedule_in: 60` and args `%{"source_id" => id, "cursor" => next_cursor}`, then return `:ok`, (3) on `{:error, reason}` return `{:error, reason}` so Oban retries the SAME cursor. Do NOT reschedule inside an error branch. Write to `lib/my_app/workers/data_fetch_worker.ex`.
tier_rationale: Tests both the reschedule-on-success-only pattern and the return-value discrimination for pagination. Rare combination.
